message:
docs(parity): note FEATURE_PARITY.md packet table is foundational subset (br-641fd / fd90.152)

fd90.152: FEATURE_PARITY.md (referenced from README as 'Detailed parity
matrix with status per feature family') has a Phase-2C Packet Evidence
table listing ~21 packets:
  - 11 FP-P2C (001-011)
  - 10 FP-P2D (025-032 + 039-040)

But actual fp-conformance coverage is 430 distinct packet suites
(verified by ls crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/ | grep -oE
'fp_p2[cd]_[0-9]+' | sort -u | wc -l). The table shows ~5% of actual
coverage — misleading users who read it expecting comprehensive status.

Auto-regenerating the table from artifacts/phase2c/ is out of scope
for a single slice. For now: add a prominent note pointing readers to
the live ledger and explaining the table is the foundational subset
(originally enumerated parity-green checkpoints from Phase 2C/2D ramp-up).

Pure docs change. No code impact. Sets up future automation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-5zni","title":"fd90.90: README test count badge says 1500+ but body says 3,200+ (badge grossly stale)","description":"fd90.74-89 README audit continuation. Top-of-README badge (line 12) claims 'tests-1500+' but body text at line 867 ('3,200+ tests across the workspace') and FAQ line 1448 ('3,200+ tests') give a much higher figure. A spot-check sums the per-crate breakdown table (lines 871-884) to ~3,392 distinct test items, and a permissive grep across #[test]/#[tokio::test]/#[rstest] returns 3,711. The badge is grossly stale relative to body text it shares the same page with. Fix: bump badge from 1500+ to 3200+ to match the lower-bound figure already cited twice in body text. Pure README change, no code impact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:02:33.001930012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:03:20.179918621Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:03:20.177282309Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-60du","title":"[HIGH] docs: CHANGELOG.md 671 commits / 12 days behind HEAD; stats table understates state by ~2-9x per row","description":"CHANGELOG.md maintenance audit.

**Observation.** `CHANGELOG.md` is 536 lines / ~48 KB. Last modified by `git log -1 -- CHANGELOG.md`: **2026-04-10 23:50:20** — "Harden IO column validation".

**Count of commits since last CHANGELOG update:** `git log --oneline <last-changelog-sha>..HEAD | wc -l` → **671 commits**.

**Stats table at the end of CHANGELOG.md (lines 527-536) is also wildly stale:**
  Total commits | 269                       ← reality: 269 + 671 = ~940
  Date range    | 2026-02-13 to 2026-03-17  ← reality extends to 2026-04-22 (today)
  Tags / releases | None (pre-release)       ← still true, but closer to 0.1.0
  Workspace crates | 12                     ← still correct
  Lines of Rust | 84,000+                    ← reality likely 120k+ given recent epics
  Public functions | 788                    ← reality: 1530 (verified 2026-04-22 via grep in br-7cfm)
  Conformance packets | 55+                  ← reality: 430 (verified 2026-04-22 in br-n609)

**Consequence.**
- CHANGELOG.md is the canonical summary a user hitting docs.rs / crates.io / GitHub Releases reads to understand what's in the project. It's 12 days + 671 commits behind reality. Every new user lands on an outdated picture.
- br-4clx (release workflow) will tag v0.1.0 with CHANGELOG.md as release-notes input. If we tag today, the notes say "55+ conformance packets" while reality is 430 — ~9x understatement.
- The CHANGELOG format (features / fixes / chore grouped by conventional-commit prefix) could be auto-generated by release-plz or git-cliff, but is currently hand-maintained. Nobody will keep up with 671-commits-every-12-days cadence by hand; the swarm's velocity outpaces manual documentation.
- br-4clx proposes release-plz for the publish workflow. release-plz IS the CHANGELOG automation — same tool. Landing 4clx closes this gap as a side-effect, but 4clx is itself blocked on br-1d9y (nightly pin), br-36qc (cargo-audit), br-0l5r (cross-platform CI). Meanwhile CHANGELOG keeps drifting.

**Severity.** HIGH — user-facing credibility at release. Also: a CHANGELOG that's stale for 12 days + 671 commits on an active swarm is effectively abandoned documentation, regardless of whether a release ships.

**Fix shape:**

1. Short-term (ship today or tomorrow): one catch-up commit. Use `git log --pretty='format:- %s'` between the last CHANGELOG sha and HEAD, filter by conventional-commit prefix (feat / fix / test / chore / docs), append to CHANGELOG under an "Unreleased" section. Regenerate the stats table using:
   - `git log --oneline | wc -l` (total commits)
   - `grep -c "^pub fn" crates/*/src/lib.rs` (public fns — use the script from br-7cfm once it ships, but a one-shot grep works for now)
   - `ls crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/*.json | wc -l` for packet count
   - `find crates -name '*.rs' | xargs wc -l` for LOC

2. Medium-term: adopt `git-cliff` (or release-plz) with a `cliff.toml` that maps conventional-commit prefixes to CHANGELOG sections. Run in CI as a "chore(changelog)" auto-commit on merge to main. Preserves the Unreleased section until `git-cliff --bump --tag v0.1.0` rolls it forward.

3. Link CHANGELOG entries to bead IDs: the project already uses `(br-frankenpandas-XXXX)` suffix on most commit subjects; `git-cliff` can extract those and link to `.beads/issues.jsonl` for traceability.

4. Once CI auto-commits the CHANGELOG, drop the hand-maintained stats table or auto-regenerate it (same script from fix 1).

Blast radius: 1 one-shot catch-up commit (~200-400 lines appended to CHANGELOG.md), +1 cliff.toml (~30 lines), +1 ci.yml job (~15 lines), +1 line in CONTRIBUTING.md (br-6d5s) pointing at the new auto-regeneration flow. Zero source-logic changes.

Prerequisites:
- None urgent for the one-shot catch-up.
- br-4clx (release workflow) for the automated flow — they should ship together.

**Related:**
- br-4clx (release workflow + release-plz) — this bead becomes moot after 4clx lands (release-plz handles changelogs natively). File separately so it can be closed quickly with a one-shot catch-up.
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — the changelog policy lives here.
- br-n609 (COVERAGE.md auto-generator) + br-zk1j (API matrix) — sibling auto-generated-doc beads. All three replace hand-maintained stats.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:16:29.279408814Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:05:29.471477679Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:05:29.469013654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["changelog","docs","release","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-63ac","title":"fd90.51: SqlReflectedTable accessor methods for columns / indexes / UCs / FKs","description":"Add accessor methods on SqlReflectedTable mirroring the SqlTableSchema::column shape: column(name) -> Option<&SqlColumnSchema>, index(name) -> Option<&SqlIndexSchema>, unique_constraint(name) -> Option<&SqlUniqueConstraintSchema>, foreign_keys_for_column(col) -> Vec<&SqlForeignKeySchema>. Pure ergonomic accessors over the named-Vec fields. No new behavior. Tests verify each accessor on a real SQLite reflection bundle and on missing names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:40:40.380451501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:43:51.235786656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:43:51.233287125Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fd90","review","sql"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-641fd","title":"fd90.152: FEATURE_PARITY.md packet evidence shows ~21 of 430 packets — add note pointing to actual ledger","description":"fd90.74-151 audit. FEATURE_PARITY.md (referenced from README's Key Documents at line 1666 as 'Detailed parity matrix with status per feature family') has a 'Phase-2C Packet Evidence' table at lines 51-75 listing only ~21 packets:
  - 11 FP-P2C packets (001-011)
  - 10 FP-P2D packets (025, 026, 027, 028, 029, 030, 031, 032, 039, 040)

But the actual fp-conformance fixture coverage is much larger:
  ls crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/ | grep -oE 'fp_p2[cd]_[0-9]+' | sort -u | wc -l
  → 430 distinct packet suites
  - 11 FP-P2C (matches README exactly)
  - 419 FP-P2D (FEATURE_PARITY.md shows 10 of 419 = 2.4% coverage)

The table grew dramatically since FEATURE_PARITY.md was last refreshed; it's now misleading users who read it expecting comprehensive parity status.

Two ways to fix:
A. Auto-regenerate the table from artifacts/phase2c/ — out of scope for a 60-min slice (would need a script).
B. Add a clarifying note: 'Below is the foundational subset; the full live packet ledger is in artifacts/phase2c/drift_history.jsonl with 430 total packet suites.'

Going with B for this slice — the README's Conformance section already gives the actual count. Pure docs-only fix to align expectations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:36:46.273393093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:38:03.259855379Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:38:03.257173150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-6d5s","title":"[HIGH] collab: no CODEOWNERS / PR template / CONTRIBUTING.md despite active multi-agent swarm — zero review-routing infrastructure","description":"Contributor / collaboration infrastructure audit.

**Observation.** `.github/` contains exactly one directory (workflows/) and nothing else. Confirmed absences:
  .github/CODEOWNERS                      — no review routing
  .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md        — no PR checklist
  .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/                 — no issue templates
  /CONTRIBUTING.md                        — no contributor guide
  /CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md                     — no code of conduct
  /SECURITY.md                            — no disclosure (br-8k1i covers separately)

**Context.** This repository is running an ACTIVE multi-agent development swarm — that's not speculation. Evidence from today's git log alone:
- 20+ commits across 6 hours from multiple agents (cc-pandas + Dicklesworthstone + parallel workers).
- br-frankenpandas-iy8u: the original bead was a doctrine note about agents bundling co-landed work with misleading titles. Closed today by c9e9fff ("docs(workflow): require pre-commit diff stat and co-landed bead IDs").
- br-frankenpandas-lxhr (monolith bead): cites rebase conflicts between parallel agents on fp-frame/src/lib.rs as a primary pain point.
- Pre-commit hooks at `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/50-agent-mail.py` prove the swarm coordinates through agent-mail infra.

Despite the swarm being active, there is NO documented process for:
- Which agent reviews which area of code (no CODEOWNERS).
- What a PR must include before merge (no PR template).
- How external contributors (humans) onboard (no CONTRIBUTING.md).
- What behavior is expected in issues / PRs (no CODE_OF_CONDUCT).
- How scientific / pandas-behavior-divergence discussions should be classified (no issue template distinguishing bug / feature / conformance-question / docs).

**Consequences already visible:**
- br-iy8u root cause (cod-style commits bundling co-landed work) persists as oral doctrine rather than enforceable CODEOWNERS routing.
- New slice-1 child bead (1zzp.1) had two agents working the same file simultaneously; the CODEOWNERS + PR template would have routed one to review the other's draft rather than both editing in parallel.
- Contributor onboarding is zero-documented: a new agent (this cc-pandas session is literally "fresh cc on new account" per the opening user message) had to reconstruct workflow from context clues.

**Severity.** HIGH — infrastructure that compounds across every PR. Every day without it, the swarm eats overhead re-discovering which file to edit and which agent to block on.

**Fix shape:**

1. `.github/CODEOWNERS`:
   - `/crates/fp-types/ @core-types-owner` (or an agent alias like `@cc-frankenpandas-types`)
   - `/crates/fp-frame/ @frame-domain`
   - `/crates/fp-io/ @io-domain`
   - `/crates/fp-conformance/ @conformance-domain`
   - `/.github/workflows/ @ci-owners`
   - `/.beads/ @scrum-bot` (if there's a bead-curator agent)
   - Auto-routes PR review requests; works with the existing multi-agent workflow.

2. `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`:
   - Required: linked bead ID (br-frankenpandas-*)
   - Required: conformance / fuzz / performance impact checkbox
   - Required: `git diff --stat` summary (directly addresses br-iy8u doctrine) — autopopulate via the pre-commit hook already in place
   - Required: which live_oracle_* tests the PR touches / exercises
   - Required: acceptance criteria from the child bead

3. `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md`, `feature_request.md`, `conformance_divergence.md`:
   - Conformance divergences get their own template because they flow to DISCREPANCIES.md, not to a regular bug tracker.

4. `CONTRIBUTING.md`:
   - How to install rch + cargo-fuzz + pandas oracle.
   - How to file a bead (`br create --no-db ...` per today's doctrine).
   - AGENTS.md link for swarm-specific workflow.
   - How to run conformance (`rch exec -- cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib`).
   - How to regenerate fixtures (once br-boyr ships the pipeline).
   - Semver and release expectations (once br-4clx ships).

5. `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`: boilerplate Contributor Covenant. Five minutes to add.

Blast radius: 5 new files, no source code changes. Each file is independently useful — can ship as 5 small PRs instead of one.

Related:
- br-frankenpandas-iy8u (workflow doctrine) — already closed via the c9e9fff commit that added pre-commit diff-stat requirement. This bead captures the codepath-level enforcement (CODEOWNERS), the human-facing documentation (CONTRIBUTING), and the PR-time enforcement (template) that the doctrine bead acknowledged were needed.
- br-frankenpandas-8k1i (SECURITY.md) — sibling file in the /github root; coordinate rollout.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:31:37.124310007Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:25:39.257863817Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:25:39.255309027Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["collab","infrastructure","review","swarm"]}

-## Phase-2C Packet Evidence (Current)
+## Phase-2C Packet Evidence (Foundational subset)
+
+> **Note:** The table below shows the FOUNDATIONAL subset of packet suites
+> (the originally enumerated parity-green checkpoints from Phase 2C/2D
+> ramp-up). The live packet ledger has expanded substantially since:
+> as of this commit, **430 distinct packet suites** exist across
+> `crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/` (11 FP-P2C + 419 FP-P2D,
+> ranging FP-P2D-014 through FP-P2D-433+). For the full real-time view,
+> see `artifacts/phase2c/drift_history.jsonl` and the per-packet
+> directories under `artifacts/phase2c/`. The README's Testing section
+> tracks the running pass/fail count.
